summary: Single-select segmented toggle family with radiogroup semantics, keyboard navigation, and three visual variants (default, inverse, pill).
philosophy: Teach SegmentedControl as the single canonical surface — no raw atom escape hatch needed since the component is already minimal.
Use radiogroup semantics for single-select behavior without associated panels, distinguishing this family from tabs.
Keep keyboard navigation (Arrow keys, Home, End) and value resolution in core so all adapters stay aligned.
Token-based styling through CSS custom properties means dark mode and theming work automatically without adapter-level logic.

useWhen: The UI needs a compact single-select toggle between 2–4 options with no associated panel content.
Theme switching, view density controls, framework selectors, or similar compact option sets.
The team wants React, Vue, and Svelte segmented behavior to stay aligned through shared core recipes.
avoidWhen: The selection controls associated panel content — use TabGroup instead.
There are more than 4 options — consider a dropdown or radio group.
A binary on/off toggle is more appropriate — use Switch instead.
The options need rich content or descriptions — use a radio group field.

automationCovers: Core recipe generates correct role=radiogroup and role=radio attributes.
Keyboard navigation (ArrowLeft, ArrowRight, Home, End) cycles through enabled items.
Disabled items are skipped during keyboard navigation.
Storybook taxonomy tests across React, Vue, and Svelte.
manualReviewRequired: Real browser and assistive-technology validation for radio-group announcement patterns.
Focus visibility and roving tabindex feel across screen readers.
High-contrast mode appearance of the active pill indicator.
notes: Initial implementation ships with radiogroup semantics rather than tablist because no panel content is associated.
Future audit should confirm that screen readers announce the group label and checked state transitions cleanly.

openQuestions: Should purpose variants (e.g. ThemeToggle, ViewDensityToggle) be added as thin wrappers?
Should the family support an animated sliding indicator for the active pill?
